epic.arc: Ontum gets its own face. The Claude Code engine and its whole environment (hooks, skills, MCP, permissions, the loop) stay — but the front door becomes an ontum-branded VS Code surface that is usable without the official Claude Code panel. The surface is a fold, never a second source: it reads the local session transcript store (~/.claude/projects/<project>/<session_id>.jsonl, named by session id) and the append-only loop log as its truth, and it drives the engine through a supported channel (the Claude Agent SDK or the headless `claude` CLI). The staging is the safety property: reach PARITY with normal Claude Code before adding any ontum meta (the per-session forest mask, attention overlay, session graph, embedded loop surfaces) or culture (the brand, naming, the surface language).
epic.context: Born from bdo, 2026-06-24, off the forest-mask plugin (PR #693): 'make my own window and remove the existing one's requirement to use it; obviously we continue to use Claude and the Claude Code environmental capabilities, and make sure we have parity with norm before we add meta and culture.' The unlock that started it: the 193 local session transcripts are files named by session id, and the file-touch sensor (.claude/hooks/file_touch.py:116) tags every record with the same session_id — so the surface owns its own session selection by reading the store, dissolving the 'VS Code won't tell a plugin which tab is selected' blocker. PR #153 first proposed epic.digital-experience ('ontum as the new digital experience') and never landed; this file re-homes it concretely. The full blueprint is .ai-native/proposals/branded-claude-surface.proposal.md. PROPOSED — awaits bdo's confirm-arc (D-4); authored to home the 2026-06-24 overnight build, not to self-authorize the arc.
epic.value: bdo works inside this window all day. Today the front door is someone else's; the loop's truth and the engine's transcripts are already on disk; the engine already exposes a supported channel to drive it. This arc makes the front door wear ontum's face — at parity first, so a branded surface that MATCHES Claude Code before it ADDS to it is a real product increment, not a demo skin.
epic.horizon: An ontum-branded VS Code surface that is the front door to the Claude engine: its own window and session list, standalone (the official panel optional or uninstalled), inheriting hooks/skills/MCP/permissions from the SDK harness, at full parity with normal Claude Code (Phase 1) — and then carrying the ontum meta (per-session mask, attention, session graph, embedded loop surfaces) and culture (brand) on top (Phase 2).

pieces.glue: CTA-2, the first step and the load-bearing unknown: prove the engine bridge. A small spike that pins the exact driving surface — Claude Agent SDK (preferred — inherits the environment) vs headless `claude` CLI stream-json (fallback) — and verifies hooks / skills / MCP / permission-modes are inherited. Its output is the honest estimate for everything else AND the gate on parity: you cannot build to parity-green over a bridge that does not exist. If both SDK and CLI prove infeasible, the arc honestly STOPS here and reports, rather than faking a green that cannot exist.

increment 1 (witness before actuator): the ontum-branded VS Code webview that READS and live-tails the selected session's transcript (~/.claude/projects/<project>/<session_id>.jsonl), with its own session list. Drives nothing, breaks nothing — proves the truth-join, the live tail, and the brand. De-risks the whole read path before any driving.

the FLOOR: drive a real turn through the bridge and build the parity checklist to green — stream a turn, render tool calls + diffs with accept/reject, permission prompts (canUseTool / permission-mode), slash commands, plan mode, @-mentions / IDE selection context, MCP, attach, resume/continue, stop/interrupt, cost/usage. The honest biggest cost; the checklist is the checkable bar, not a feeling. Phase 1 is done when this is green.

Phase 2, named-not-built (gated on parity-green): the per-session forest mask scoped via the session-id join, the attention/context overlay, the session graph, and embedded loop surfaces (meta) — then the ontum brand, naming, and surface language (culture) over all of it. Deliberately deferred behind the parity gate (do not stack overlays until the floor matches the norm).
